commands.review_summary:
Adds a bounded SDK fetch wrapper for successful SSE responses, wires it into the `openai-responses` OpenAI client, and adds helper/provider tests.

PR surface: Source +103, Tests +202. Total +305 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current `main` uses the OpenAI SDK without a custom fetch while the SDK accumulates SSE chunks until a delimiter; this review did not run a live hostile endpoint.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer acceptance of the compatibility tradeoff and contributor-supplied real behavior proof; ClawSweeper cannot generate proof of the contributor’s runtime setup for them.

target.title: fix(openai-responses): bound SSE response body reads at 16 MiB
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The openai-responses provider creates an OpenAI SDK client without a custom `fetch` option, so all SSE streaming responses from the OpenAI API are read without any byte cap. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning `text/event-stream` with a large or never-ending body is fully buffered into memory by the SDKs internal HTTP layer — an OOM / DoS vector.

This applies the `createSdkBoundedFetch()` wrapper at `src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts:171` to cap SSE response body reads at 16 MiB, matching the existing bounded-read pattern used by the transport-stream path (`openai-transport-stream.ts:2016`).

## Changes

- `src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.ts` — new shared `createSdkBoundedFetch()` function that wraps `fetch` to cap SSE response body reads at 16 MiB. Non-SSE and error-status responses pass through unchanged.
- `src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.test.ts` — 9 tests: happy path, overflow with upstream cancel, non-SSE passthrough, error-status passthrough, content-type matching.
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts:171` — inject `fetch: createSdkBoundedFetch()` into the OpenAI SDK constructor.
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts` — 2 integration tests verifying the bounded fetch through the production wrapper.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: SSE response body reads in the openai-responses provider are now bounded at 16 MiB. An oversized SSE response triggers a stream error and cancels the upstream connection.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 22, vitest 4.1.8
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  pnpm test src/agents/sdk-bounded-fetch.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses.test.ts --run
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  11 passed (11)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: Oversized SSE response (1536 bytes body, 1024 byte cap) → stream error after 2 chunks (1536 read), upstream cancelled: true, pulls limited to 2 out of 6.
- **What was not tested**: Did not test live OpenAI API endpoint (would not reproduce a hostile oversized body — mock fetch is the correct abstraction for this assertion).

## Out of scope

Symmetric PRs for azure-openai-responses and openai-completions are submitted separately (A2, A3).

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? No — existing request flows continue to work. Only oversized SSE responses (>16 MiB) trigger a new stream error.
- Config/env/migration change? No.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? Yes — OOM/DoS protection for SSE response handling.
- Highest-risk area: false positive on valid large SSE payloads. The 16 MiB cap aligns with `PROVIDER_JSON_RESPONSE_MAX_BYTES` in `provider-http-errors.ts` and matches the transport-stream path. Non-SSE responses are unaffected.
